Reference_title: Constitutive expression of AtSINA2 from Arabidopsis improves grain yield, seed oil and drought tolerance in transgenic soybean
Description: Overexpression of AtSINA2 in Arabidopsis positively regulated the drought tolerance of transgenic plants. Ectopic expression of AtSINA2 in soybean improved the shoot growth, grain yield, drought tolerance and seed oil content in transgenic plants. Compared to wild type, transgenic soybean produced greater shoot biomass and grain yield, and showed improved seed oil and drought tolerance. Functions as positive regulator of abscisic acid-mediated stomatal closure.

Reference: Mehdi, S. M. M., Szczesniak, M. W., & Ludwików, A. (2023)
Reference_title: The Bro1-like domain-containing protein, AtBro1, modulates growth and abiotic stress responses in Arabidopsis.
Description: AtBro1 transcripts were upregulated by salt, ABA and mannitol stress, while AtBro1-overexpression lines demonstrated robust tolerance to drought and salt stress. Furthermore, we found that ABA elicits stress-resistance responses in loss-of-function bro1-1 mutant plants and AtBro1 regulates drought resistance in Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: CARK3-mediated ADF4 regulates hypocotyl elongation and soil drought stress in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of ADF4 markedly enhance drought resistance in soil when compared with WT. On the other hand, drought tolerance analysis showed that overexpression of CARK3 could rescue adf4 drought susceptibility. CARK3 acts as a regulator in hypocotyl elongation and drought tolerance likely via regulating ADF4 phosphorylation.

Synonyms: Haloacid dehalogenase-like hydrolase (HAD) superfamily protein, T31P16_90
Reference_title: Arabidopsis thaliana trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase gene TPPI enhances drought tolerance by regulating stomatal apertures.
Description: Trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase which enhances drought tolerance by regulating stomatal apertures. AtTPPI, confers drought resistance and improves WUE by decreasing stomatal apertures and improving root architecture. The reduced expression of AtTPPI caused a drought-sensitive phenotype, while its overexpression significantly increased drought tolerance. Yeast one-hybrid assays showed that ABA-responsive element-binding factor1 (ABF1), ABF2, and ABF4 directly regulated AtTPPI expression.

Reference_title: An Arabidopsis E3 ligase HUB 2 increases histone H2B monoubiquitination and enhances drought tolerance in transgenic cotton.
Description: Encodes one of two orthologous E3 ubiquitin ligases in Arabidopsis that are involved in monoubiquitination of histone H2B. AtHUB2 plays a positive role in Arabidopsis under drought stress conditions. AtHUB2 overexpression improves the agricultural traits of transgenic cotton grown in a waterproof shed. AtHUB2 overexpression in cotton enhances drought tolerance and regulates drought stress-related gene expression in the greenhouse.

Synonyms: Protein with RING/U-box and TRAF-like domain, RING-type E3 ubiquitin transferase
Reference_title: Over-expression of SINAL7 increases biomass and drought tolerance, and also delays senescence in Arabidopsis.
Description: SINAL7 lines had reduced stomatal apertures and showed delayed senescence. Over-expression of SINAL7 greatly improved drought tolerance in Arabidopsis. Over-expression of SINAL7 resulted in an increase in the concentrations of hexoses and sucrose, with a concommitant increase in plant biomass, particularly in the number of rosette leaves and stem thickness.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis MAPKKK18 positively regulates drought stress resistance via downstream MAPKK3.
Description: Arabidopsis MAPKKK18 enhances drought resistance via accelerating stomatal closure. MAPKKK18 is induced by osmotic stress in an ABA-dependent manner. MAPKK3, a downstream target of MAPKKK18, is a positive regulator in drought stress resistance.

Reference_title: The karrikin receptor KAI2 promotes drought resistance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: KAI2 promotes ABA responses and ABA catabolism. KAI2-mediated signaling positively regulates drought resistance in Arabidopsis. KAI2 is activated through the binding of KARs (or as-yet-unknown KL), resulting in the regulation of downstream genes involved in cuticle formation, stomatal closure, anthocyanin biosynthesis and membrane integrity.

Reference_title: The karrikin receptor KAI2 promotes drought resistance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: D14 acts as a positive regulator of drought resistance. d14-2 had a higher reduction in biomass than WT in the gravimetric method-based drought-resistance assay. Both D14- and KAI2-mediated signaling pathways may act together, perhaps through MAX2 to enhance drought resistance in Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: A dual-function transcription factor, AtYY1, is a novel negative regulator of the Arabidopsis ABA response network.
Description: Binds to ABR1 promoter and promotes its expression, thus negatively regulating the abscisic acid (ABA) signaling pathway. The yy1 mutant was more sensitive to ABA and NaCl than the wild-type, while overexpressing AtYY1 plants were less sensitive. AtYY1 loss also enhanced ABA-induced stomatal closing and drought resistance.

Reference: Kim, D., Ntui, V. O., & Xiong, L. (2016).
Reference_title: Arabidopsis YAK 1 regulates abscisic acid response and drought resistance.
Description: AtYak1 knockout mutant plants were hyposensitive to ABA inhibition of seed germination, cotyledon greening, seedling growth, and stomatal movement. atyak1-1 mutant plants display reduced drought stress resistance, as evidenced by water loss rate and survival rate. AtYak1 plays a role as a positive regulator in ABA-mediated drought response in Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: The heat stress factor HSFA6b connects ABA signaling and ABA-mediated heat responses.
Description: HSFA6b Overexpression Conferred Drought and Salt Tolerance. HSFA6b, a Nuclear Factor, Responds to ABA Signaling via the AREB/ABF-ABRE Regulon. HSFA6b, a Positive Regulator, Is Involved in ABA-Mediated Drought, Salt, and Thermotolerance.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis EDT 1/HDG 11 improves drought and salt tolerance in cotton and poplar and increases cotton yield in the field.
Description: Transcription factor which acts as positive regulator of drought stress tolerance. Overexpression of AtHDG11 in cotton enhances drought tolerance in laboratory condition. AtHDG11 enhances tolerance to salinity stress in cotton. Reduced leaf stomatal density and increased water-use efficiency in AtHDG11-expressing cotton.

Reference: Shi, H., Ye, T., Yang, F., & Chan, Z. (2015).
Reference_title: Arabidopsis PED2 positively modulates plant drought stress resistance.
Description: AtPED2 plays a positive role in drought resistance, through modulation of stress-responsive genes expression, ROS metabolism, and metabolic homeostasis. Multiple carbon metabolites including amino acids, organic acids, sugars, sugar alcohols, and aromatic amines were also positively regulated by AtPED2.

Synonyms: OBF4, OCS ELEMENT BINDING FACTOR 4, OCTOPINE SYNTHASE ELEMENT-BINDING PROTEIN
Reference_title: AtTGA4, a bZIP transcription factor, confers drought resistance by enhancing nitrate transport and assimilation in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: Overexpression of AtTGA4 improved drought resistance in transgenic Arabidopsis. Higher levels of nitrogen transport and assimilation activity contributed to enhanced drought resistance in transgenic AtTGA4 plants.

Reference_title: Overexpression of Arabidopsis MYB96 confers drought resistance in Camelina sativa via cuticular wax accumulation.
Description: Transgenic plants overexpressing Arabidopsis MYB96 exhibited normal growth and development and enhanced tolerance to drought. Deposition of epicuticular wax crystals and total wax loads increased significantly on the surfaces of transgenic leaves compared with that of non-transgenic plants. Encodes a R2R3 type Myb transcription factor whose expression is strongly induced by abscisic acid. Mediates abscisic acid signaling during drought stress response. Promotes seed fatty acid accumulation.

Gene name: Leaf rust 10 disease-resistance locus receptor-like protein kinase 1
Synonyms: Serine/Threonine kinase family catalytic domain protein
Reference_title: The AtLRK10L1. 2, Arabidopsis ortholog of wheat LRK10, is involved in ABA-mediated signaling and drought resistance.
Description: The loss-of-function mutants of the Arabidopsis LRK10 gene shows ABA-insensitive and drought stress-sensitive phenotypes, suggesting that LRK10L1.2 is positively involved in ABA signaling.

Synonyms: AIF, ANAC053, ANTHER INDEHISCENCE FACTOR, NAC053
Reference_title: A NAC transcription factor NTL4 promotes reactive oxygen species production during drought‐induced leaf senescence in Arabidopsis.
Description: a drought-responsive NAC transcription factor NTL4 promotes ROS production by binding directly to the promoters of genes encoding ROS biosynthetic enzymes during drought-induced leaf senescence. ROS levels were reduced in NTL4-deficient ntl4 mutants, which exhibited delayed leaf senescence and enhanced drought resistance.

Reference_title: Activation of a flavin monooxygenase gene YUCCA7 enhances drought resistance in Arabidopsis.
Description: Encodes a flavin monooxygenase gene which belongs to the tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthetic pathway and enhances drought resistance. The YUC7 gene was induced by drought, primarily in the roots, in an abscisic acid (ABA)-dependent manner.

Synonyms: ATPDR8, PEN3, PENETRATION 3, PLEIOTROPIC DRUG RESISTANCE 8
Reference_title: Overexpression of AtABCG36 improves drought and salt stress resistance in Arabidopsis.
Description: AtABCG36-overexpressing plants were more resistant to drought and salt stress and grew to higher shoot fresh weight (FW) than the wild-type. AtABCG36 contributes to drought and salt resistance in Arabidopsis by a mechanism that includes reduction of sodium content in plants.

Reference_title: Drought tolerance in Arabidopsis is controlled by the OCP3 disease resistance regulator.
Description: The Arabidopsis loss-of-function ocp3 mutant exhibits both drought resistance and enhanced disease resistance to necrotrophic fungal pathogens. Loss of ABA biosynthesis and perception suppresses all major aspects of drought resistance in ocp3 plants. OCP3 is a negative regulator in a specific branch of the ABA-mediated drought-response network controlling stomatal movement.

Synonyms: RCAR8, REGULATORY COMPONENT OF ABA RECEPTOR 8
Reference_title: Modulation of drought resistance by the abscisic acid receptor PYL5 through inhibition of clade A PP2Cs.
Description: PYL5 over-expression led to a globally enhanced response to ABA. PYL5 inhibited HAB1, ABI1 and ABI2 phosphatase activity in an ABA-dependent manner. Enhanced resistance to drought can be obtained through PYL5-mediated inhibition of clade A PP2Cs.

Reference: Wohlbach, D. J., Quirino, B. F., & Sussman, M. R. (2008).
Reference_title: Analysis of the Arabidopsis histidine kinase ATHK1 reveals a connection between vegetative osmotic stress sensing and seed maturation.
Description: Overexpression of ATHK1 results in increased water stress tolerance, our observations suggest a new top-down route to increasing drought resistance via receptor-mediated increases in sensing water status. athk1 null mutants showed significantly more sensitivity to drought. Overexpression of ATHK1 increased tolerance to drought, osmotic, and salinity stresses. Positive regulator of drought and salt stress responses, and abscisic acid (ABA) signaling. Confers drought tolerance, probably by regulating levels of ABA accumulation.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis SDIR1 enhances drought tolerance in crop plants. Arabidopsis SDIR1 gene plays a positive role in ABA signaling and promotes the ABA-mediated drought tolerant phenotype when overexpressed in Arabidopsis
Description: SDIR1-overexpressing Arabidopsis plants exhibit improved tolerance to drought. Ectopic expression of SDIR1 in both tobacco and rice plants conferred improved drought tolerance ability. These results suggest that SDIR1 can function as a drought-tolerance gene in both dicotyledons and monocotyledons.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis NFYA5 transcription factor is regulated transcriptionally and posttranscriptionally to promote drought resistance.
Description: NFYA5 transcript is strongly induced by drought stress in an abscisic acid (ABA)-dependent manner. NFYA5 contains a target site for miR169, which targets mRNAs for cleavage or translational repression. nfya5 knockout plants and plants overexpressing miR169a showed enhanced leaf water loss and were more sensitive to drought stress than wild-type plants. NFYA5 is important for drought resistance, and its induction by drought stress occurs at both the transcriptional and posttranscriptional levels.

Synonyms: Ethylene-responsive transcription factor ERF024, Integrase-type DNA-binding superfamily protein
Reference_title: Improvement of water use efficiency in rice by expression of HARDY, an Arabidopsis drought and salt tolerance gene.
Description: Overexpression of HRD increases the density of the root network and improves water and salt stress tolerance in Arabidopsis. Overexpression of HRD in rice causes an increase in plant biomass and improves drought resistance and WUE. The HRD gene, an AP2/ERF-like transcription factor, identified by a gain-of-function Arabidopsis mutant hrd-D having roots with enhanced strength, branching, and cortical cells, exhibits drought resistance and salt tolerance, accompanied by an enhancement in the expression of abiotic stress associated genes.

Reference_title: Transcription factor CBF4 is a regulator of drought adaptation in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of CBF4 in transgenic Arabidopsis plants results in the activation of C-repeat/dehydration-responsive element containing downstream genes that are involved in cold acclimation and drought adaptation. CBF4 Overexpression Results in Plants That Are More Freezing and Drought Tolerant.

Reference_title: Comparative proteomics and metabolomics of JAZ7-mediated drought tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of AtJAZ7 in Arabidopsis improves drought tolerance by regulating photosynthesis, redox, antioxidant, and metabolic pathways involving amino acids, plant hormones, and defense metabolites.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis Cys2/His2 zinc finger transcription factor ZAT18 is a positive regulator of plant tolerance to drought stress.
Description: Overexpression (OE) of ZAT18 in Arabidopsis improved drought tolerance while mutation of ZAT18 resulted in decreased plant tolerance to drought stress. ZAT18 OE plants exhibited less leaf water loss, lower content of reactive oxygen species (ROS), higher leaf water content, and higher antioxidant enzyme activities after drought treatment when compared with the wild type (WT).

Reference_title: AtCPK23 functions in Arabidopsis responses to drought and salt stresses.
Description: AtCPK23 improved the drought tolerance in Arabidopsis by behaving as a negative regulator of abiotic stress signaling. The results of stomatal aperture measurement showed that the disruption of AtCPK23 expression reduced stomatal apertures, while overexpression of AtCPK23 increased stomatal apertures.

Reference_title: AtCPK6, a functionally redundant and positive regulator involved in salt/drought stress tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Description: Over-expression of AtCPK6 in Arabidopsis led to enhanced drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis calcium-dependent protein kinase CPK10 functions in abscisic acid-and Ca2+-mediated stomatal regulation in response to drought stress.
Description: CPK10 overexpression lines displayed enhanced tolerance to drought stress. Induction of stomatal closure and inhibition of stomatal opening by abscisic acid (ABA) and Ca(2+) were impaired in the cpk10 mutants. Evidences also indicated the participation of AtCPK10 in ABA-responsive drought tolerance.

Reference_title: The transcription factor WIN1/SHN1 regulates cutin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: Overexpression of WIN1/SHN1 increases wax production, modulate cuticular permeability, and enhances the drought tolerance in Arabidopsis. WIN1/SHN encodes an APETALA2/EREBP-type transcription factor.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis Ca2+‐dependent protein kinase CPK12 negatively regulates abscisic acid signaling in seed germination and post‐germination growth.
Description: AtCPK12 has been categorized as a negative ABA signaling pathway regulator

Reference_title: Expression of a monothiol glutaredoxin, AtGRXS17, in tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) enhances drought tolerance.
Description: Ectopic expression of AtGRXS17 enhances drought stress tolerance in tomato. AtGRXS17-expressing tomato plants reduce excessive ROS accumulation.

Reference_title: Heterologous Expression of AtWRKY57 Confers Drought Tolerance in Oryza sativa.
Description: Overexpressing AtWRKY57 enhanced drought tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana. Arabidopsis WRKY57 transcription factor was able to confer drought tolerance to transgenic rice (Oryza sativa) plants. The enhanced drought tolerance of transgenic rice was resulted from the lower water loss rates, cell death, malondialdehyde contents and relative electrolyte leakage while a higher proline content and reactive oxygen species-scavenging enzyme activities was observed during stress conditions.

Reference_title: Phosphatase AtDBP1 negatively regulates drought and salt tolerance through altering leaf surface permeability in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of AtDBP1 negatively regulates drought stress by decreasing cuticle wax accumulation and cuticle membrane thickness.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis SAP5 functions as a positive regulator of stress responses and exhibits E3 ubiquitin ligase activity.
Description: AtSAP5 functions as an E3 ubiquitin ligase and interacts with C-MYC BINDING PROTEIN (AtMBP-1), promoting its proteolysis, and therefore plays a role as a positive regulator of drought stress responses.

Reference_title: AtbHLH68 transcription factor contributes to the regulation of ABA homeostasis and drought stress tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: AtbHLH68 overexpressing lines displayed defects in lateral root (LR) formation and a significant increased tolerance to drought stress, likely related to an enhanced sensitivity to abscisic acid (ABA) and/or increased ABA content.

Synonyms: RCAR1, REGULATORY COMPONENT OF ABA RECEPTOR 1
Reference_title: ABA receptor PYL9 promotes drought resistance and leaf senescence.
Description: PYL9 promotes drought resistance by not only limiting transpirational water loss but also, causing summer dormancy-like responses, such as senescence, in old leaves and growth inhibition in young tissues under severe drought conditions. PYL9 Confers Drought Resistance to Both Arabidopsis and Rice.

Reference_title: Overexpression of AtMYB44 enhances stomatal closure to confer abiotic stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: Transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing AtMYB44 is more sensitive to ABA and has a more rapid ABA-induced stomatal closure response than wild-type and atmyb44 knockout plants. Transgenic plants exhibited a reduced rate of water loss, as measured by the fresh-weight loss of detached shoots, and remarkably enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stress compared to wild-type plants.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Transcription Factors Are Involved in Brassinosteroid-Regulated Plant Growth and Drought Responses.
Description: WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Play Negative Roles in the Drought Response and Repress Dehydration-Inducible Gene Expression. WRKY54 and BES1 Cooperate to Negatively Regulate Dehydration-Induced Genes.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Transcription Factors Are Involved in Brassinosteroid-Regulated Plant Growth and Drought Responses.
Description: WRKY transcription factor 46 play positive role in brassinosteroid-regulated growth and a negative role in drought response by cooperating with BES1. WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Play Negative Roles in the Drought Response and Repress Dehydration-Inducible Gene Expression.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Transcription Factors Are Involved in Brassinosteroid-Regulated Plant Growth and Drought Responses.
Description: WRKY transcription factor 70 play positive role in brassinosteroid-regulated growth and a negative role in drought response by cooperating with BES1. WRKY46, WRKY54, and WRKY70 Play Negative Roles in the Drought Response and Repress Dehydration-Inducible Gene Expression.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis DREB2A-interacting proteins function as RING E3 ligases and negatively regulate plant drought stress-responsive gene expression.
Description: AtDRIP2 functions as negative regulator of drought stress response.

Reference_title: JUNGBRUNNEN1 Confers Drought Tolerance Downstream of the HD-Zip I Transcription Factor AtHB13.
Description: JUB1 is a drought-induced gene whose overexpression enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis. AtHB13 Binds to the JUB1 Promoter and Activates Its Expression during Drought Stress. JUB1 directly regulates the expression of stress-responsive TFs such as DREB2A and it reduces the cellular levels of reactive oxygen species (ROS), which contributes to the enhanced stress tolerance

Reference: Cabello, J. V., & Chan, R. L. 2012
Reference_title: The homologous homeodomain-leucine zipper transcription factors HaHB1 and AtHB13 confer tolerance to drought and salinity stresses via the induction of proteins that stabilize membranes.
Description: Ectopic expression of AtHB13 confers tolerance to drought and is associated with increased yield.

Reference_title: Overexpression of the AtbZIP60 gene in transgenic cell lines improved salt, drought, and cold stress tolerances by regulating expression of Ca2+-dependent protein kinase genes.
Description: AtbZIP60 gene enhanced salt, drought, and cold tolerance in rice and white pine transgenic cell lines. In rice, transcription factor AtbZIP60 increased expression of Ca2+-dependent protein kinase genes OsCPK6, OsCPK9, OsCPK10, OsCPK19, OsCPK25, and OsCPK26 under treatment of salt, drought, and cold.

Reference_title: COST1 regulates autophagy to control plant drought tolerance.
Description: Overexpression of COST1 leads to drought hypersensitivity and decreased autophagy in response to stress. COST1 negatively regulates autophagy, and aggregation/degradation of COST1 during drought therefore activates autophagy, allowing plants to tolerate drought conditions.

Reference_title: A phloem-localized Arabidopsis metacaspase (AtMC3) improves drought tolerance.
Description: Overexpression of AtMC3 conferred drought tolerance by enhancing the differentiation of specific vascular tissues and maintaining higher levels of vascular-mediated transportation, while plants lacking the protein showed an impaired response to drought and inability to respond effectively to the hormone abscisic acid.

Reference_title: Overexpression of Arabidopsis acyl-CoA-binding protein ACBP2 enhances drought tolerance.
Description: ACBP2 was induced by ABA and drought, and transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing ACBP2 (ACBP2-OXs) showed increased sensitivity to ABA treatment during germination and seedling development. ACBP2-OXs also displayed improved drought tolerance and ABA-mediated reactive oxygen species (ROS) production in guard cells, thereby promoting stomatal closure, reducing water loss and enhancing drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Overexpression of AtMYB52 confers ABA hypersensitivity and drought tolerance.
Description: AtMYB52 overexpression lines were drought-tolerant and their seedlings were salt-sensitive.

Reference_title: SUMO E3 ligase AtMMS21 regulates drought tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana(F).
Description: AtMMS21 gene negatively regulates drought tolerance and constitutive expression of MMS21 reduces drought tolerance. Under drought conditions, mms21 mutants showed the highest survival rate and the slowest water loss, and accumulated a higher level of free proline compared to wild-type (WT) and MMS21 over-expression plants.

Reference_title: Drought-induced AtbZIP62 transcription factor regulates drought stress response in Arabidopsis
Description: AtbZIP62 could be involved in AtPYD1 transcriptional regulation while modulating cellular redox state and photosynthetic processes. In addition, AtbZIP62 is suggested to positively regulate drought stress response in Arabidopsis. Both atbzip62 and atpyd1-2 mutants showed high sensitivity towards drought stress suggesting positive regulation by AtbZIP62.

Reference_title: GBF3 transcription factor imparts drought tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: Overexpression of AtGBF3 in A. thaliana resulted in improved tolerance to osmotic stress, salinity and drought stress in addition to conferring insensitivity to ABA.

Gene name: DNA-binding protein with MIZ/SP-RING zinc finger
Reference_title: The Arabidopsis E3 SUMO ligase SIZ1 regulates plant growth and drought responses.
Description: SIZ1 Is a Positive Regulator of Drought Stress Tolerance. SIZ1 Regulates Both Basal and Drought-Inducible Gene Expression.

Reference_title: AtUGT76C2, an Arabidopsis cytokinin glycosyltransferase is involved in drought stress adaptation.
Description: UGT76C2 overexpression enhances drought stress tolerance during adult stage. UGT76C2 is involved in affecting the stomata movement under drought stress and ABA treatment

Reference_title: Regulation of drought tolerance by the F-box protein MAX2 in Arabidopsis.
Description: max2 mutant is hypersensitive to drought stress compared with the wild type

Reference_title: Overexpression of Arabidopsis aspartic protease APA1 gene confers drought tolerance.
Description: Overexpressing APA1 Arabidopsis plants (OE-APA1), showed a phenotype more tolerant to drought compared with WT.

Gene name: Arginine Glycine Glycine (RGG) box-containing RNA-binding protein
Reference_title: The Arabidopsis RNA-binding protein AtRGGA regulates tolerance to salt and drought stress.
Description: When drought or salt stress was imposed, the rgga mutant appeared more sensitive than the wild type, with a more obvious bleaching of leaves. In contrast, overexpressing plants showed less wilting and bleaching symptoms in the presence of salt and drought stress compared with Col-0.

Reference_title: Overexpression of the aspartic protease ASPG1 gene confers drought avoidance in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of the ASPG1 gene enhanced ABA sensitivity in guard cells and reduced water loss in ectopically overexpressing ASPG1 (ASPG1-OE) transgenic plants. ASPG1 confers drought avoidance through the ABA signal transduction pathway.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis thaliana integrin-like gene AT14A improves drought tolerance in Solanum lycopersicum.
Description: AT14A regulates expression of Protein phosphatase 2 C 51 (PP2C 51) and ABSCISIC ACID-INSENSITIVE 5 (ABI5) to participate in ABA pathways to enhance drought tolerance. AT14A enhanced drought tolerance by regulating waxy cuticle synthesis genes. AT14A improves drought tolerance of transgenic tomato.

Reference_title: Histone deacetylase HDA9 negatively regulates salt and drought stress responsiveness in Arabidopsis.
Description: HDA9 might be a novel chromatin protein that negatively regulates plant sensitivity to salt and drought stresses by regulating histone acetylation levels of a large number of stress-responsive genes in Arabidopsis.

Gene name: SUGAR WILL EVENTUALLY BE EXPORTED TRANSPORTER 17
Reference_title: Vacuolar fructose transporter SWEET17 is critical for root development and drought tolerance.
Description: sweet17 knock-out mutants exhibited reduced lateral root (LR) growth and a diminished expression of LR-development-related transcription factors during drought stress, resulting in impaired drought tolerance of sweet17 mutants. SWEET17 loss-of-function mutants exhibit decreased tolerance against drought stress and increased drought-dependent fructose accumulation.

Reference_title: Activated expression of AtWRKY53 negatively regulates drought tolerance by mediating stomatal movement.
Description: AtWRKY53 overexpression lines were hypersensitive to drought stress compared with Col-0 plants. WRKY53 regulated stomatal movement via reducing the H2O2 content and promoting the starch metabolism in guard cells.

Reference_title: The MYB96 transcription factor regulates cuticular wax biosynthesis under drought conditions in Arabidopsis.
Description: MYB96 transcription factor confers drought resistance by modulating cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis SMALL AUXIN UP RNA32 Protein Regulates ABA-Mediated Responses to Drought Stress.
Description: AtSAUR32 has the potential to diminish the negative impact of drought stress. AtSAUR32 overexpression enhanced drought tolerance in Arabidopsis while AtSAUR32 knockout plants displayed contrasting results.

Reference_title: A novel thiol-reductase activity of Arabidopsis YUC6 confers drought tolerance independently of auxin biosynthesis.
Description: Arabidopsis plants overexpressing YUC6 display enhanced IAA-related phenotypes and exhibit improved drought stress tolerance, low rate of water loss and controlled ROS accumulation under drought and oxidative stresses.

Reference_title: BIL9 Promotes Both Plant Growth via BR Signaling and Drought Stress Resistance by Binding with the Transcription Factor HDG11.
Description: Overexpression of BIL9 enhances drought and mannitol stress resistance and increases the expression of drought-responsive genes. BIL9 protein is induced by dehydration and interacts with the HD-Zip IV transcription factor HOMEODOMAIN GLABROUS 11 (HDG11), which is known to promote plant resistance to drought stress, in vitro and in vivo.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis galactinol synthase AtGolS2 improves drought tolerance in the monocot model Brachypodium distachyon.
Description: AtGolS2-expressing transgenics displayed significantly improved drought tolerance, increasing with increased expression of AtGolS2.

Reference_title: The Arabidopsis GTL1 transcription factor regulates water use efficiency and drought tolerance by modulating stomatal density via transrepression of SDD1.
Description: AtGTL1 negatively regulates water use efficiency by modulating stomatal density; the mutation of the encoding gene increases the plant tolerance to drought stress

Reference_title: SUPPRESSOR of MAX2 1 (SMAX1) and SMAX1-LIKE2 (SMXL2) Negatively Regulate Drought Resistance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: SMAX1 and SMXL2 negatively regulate drought resistance, and disruption of these KAR- and SL-signaling-related genes may therefore provide a novel means for improving crop drought resistance.

Reference_title: SUPPRESSOR of MAX2 1 (SMAX1) and SMAX1-LIKE2 (SMXL2) Negatively Regulate Drought Resistance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: SMAX1 and SMXL2 negatively regulate drought resistance, and disruption of these KAR- and SL-signaling-related genes may therefore provide a novel means for improving crop drought resistance.

Reference_title: Arabidopsis 3-ketoacyl-CoA thiolase-2 (KAT2), an enzyme of fatty acid Î²-oxidation, is involved in ABA signal transduction
Description: kat2-3 mutant: ABA-insensitive phenotypes, KAT2-overexpressing transgenic lines: strong ABA-hypersensitive phenotypes (ABA-induced stomatal closure and  inhibition of stomatal opening

Reference_title: PDR-type ABC transporter mediates cellular uptake of the phytohormone abscisic acid
Description: knockout: upregulation of ABA responsive genes is delayed, stomata close more slowly

Reference_title: ABC transporter AtABCG25 is involved in abscisic acid transport and responses
Description: overexpressor: higher leaf temperature

Reference_title: Disruption of AtMRP4, a guard cell plasma membrane ABCC-type ABC transporter, leads to deregulation of stomatal opening and increased drought susceptibility
Description: knockout: increased stomatal aperture, increased water loss

Reference_title: Homeodomain protein ATHB6 is a target of the protein phosphatase ABI1 and regulates hormone responses in Arabidopsis
Description: overexpression: ABA insensitivity; water loss in leaves from overexpressing lines increased

Reference_title: Two cap-binding proteins CBP20 and CBP80 are involved in processing primary MicroRNAs
Description: loss-of-function: hypersensitivity to ABA; enhanced drought tolerance

Gene name: Kinase-like (open stomata 1), activated by ABA, activates SLAC1
Reference_title: Arabidopsis OST1 protein kinase mediates the regulation of stomatal aperture by abscisic acid and acts upstream of reactive oxygen species production
Description: ABA-induced stomatal closure is impaired in ost1

Reference_title: A protein phosphatase 2C involved in ABA signal transduction in Arabidopsis thaliana
Description: ABA insensitive mutant: wilting and witherung under water stress condition

Reference_title: ABI2, a second protein phosphatase 2C involved in abscisic acid signal transduction in Arabidopsis
Description: ABA insensitive

Reference_title: Regulation of drought tolerance by gene manipulation of 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase, a key enzyme in abscisic acid biosynthesis in Arabidopsis.
Description: overexpressor: improved drought tolerance; antisense: drought-sensitive phenotype

Gene name: Arabidopsis aldehyde oxidase, catalyzes final step in ABA biosynthesis
Reference_title: The Arabidopsis aldehyde oxidase 3 (AAO3) gene product catalyzes the final step in abscisic acid biosynthesis in leaves
Description: mutant: wilty phenotype

Reference_title: High humidity induces abscisic acid 8'-hydroxylase in stomata and vasculature to regulate local and systemic abscisic acid responses in Arabidopsis
Description: transcript increases in response to salt, osmotic, dehydration stress and ABA; stomatal closure of the cyp707a1 mutant was ABA hypersensitive when epidermal peel was treated with exogenous ABA

Gene name: beta-glucosidase; hydrolyzes glucose-conjugated, inactive ABA to active ABA
Reference_title: Activation of glucosidase via stress-induced polymerization rapidly increases active pools of abscisic acid
Description: overexpression: drought tolerance

Reference_title: Two novel GPCR-type G proteins are abscisic acid receptors in Arabidopsis
Description: ABA induction of stomatal closure the gtg1 gtg2 mutant plants is ABA hyposensitive

Reference_title: Two novel GPCR-type G proteins are abscisic acid receptors in Arabidopsis
Description: ABA induction of stomatal closure the gtg1 gtg2 mutant plants is ABA hyposensitive

Reference_title: High humidity induces abscisic acid 8'-hydroxylase in stomata and vasculature to regulate local and systemic abscisic acid responses in Arabidopsis
Description: cyp707a3 mutant exhibited high ABA levels even after transferring to high-humidity conditions

Reference_title: Constitutive activation of a plasma membrane H(+)-ATPase prevents abscisic acid-mediated stomatal closure
Description: mutant: reduced ability to close stomata in response to drought; downregulated during drought

Gene name: light harvesting chlorophyll a/b binding protein
Reference_title: Light-harvesting chlorophyll a/b-binding proteins are required for stomatal response to abscisic acid in Arabidopsis
Description: mutant: reduced resposiveness to ABA-dependent stomatal movement, decreased drought tolerance; overexpression: stomatal sensitivity to ABA is enhanced

Gene name: osmotic stess-sensitive; related to mammalian syntaxin, SNARE family protein
Reference_title: OSM1/SYP61: a syntaxin protein in Arabidopsis controls abscisic acid-mediated and non-abscisic acid-mediated responses to abiotic stress
